trans-unit.source: Geodata Import in QGIS
trans-unit.target: Geodata Import in QGIS

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: Vector data Import
trans-unit.target: Vector data Import

trans-unit.source: Open vector layer
trans-unit.target: Open vector layer

trans-unit.source: Open vector data via Layer Tab
trans-unit.target: Open vector data via Layer Tab

trans-unit.source: Click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to your vector file.
trans-unit.target: Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to your vector file.

trans-unit.source: Select the file and click `Open`
trans-unit.target: Select the file and click `Open`

trans-unit.source: Back in QGIS click `Add`
trans-unit.target: Back in QGIS click `Add`

trans-unit.source: Open vector data via drag-and-drop
trans-unit.target: Open vector data via drag-and-drop

trans-unit.source: In your file browser, open the folder with the vector data you want to import.
trans-unit.target: In your file browser, open the folder with the vector data you want to import.

trans-unit.source: Drag-and-drop it onto the QGIS map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Drag-and-drop it onto the QGIS map canvas.

trans-unit.source: Raster data Import
trans-unit.target: Raster data Import

trans-unit.source: Open raster data via Layer Tab
trans-unit.target: Open raster data via Layer Tab

trans-unit.source: Click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Raster Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Raster Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to your raster file.
trans-unit.target: Click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to your raster file.

trans-unit.source: Select the file and click `Open`
trans-unit.target: Select the file and click `Open`

trans-unit.source: Back in QGIS click `Add`
trans-unit.target: Back in QGIS click `Add`

trans-unit.source: Open raster data via drag-and-drop
trans-unit.target: Open raster data via drag-and-drop

trans-unit.source: In your file browser, open the folder with the raster data you want to import.
trans-unit.target: In your file browser, open the folder with the raster data you want to import.

trans-unit.source: Drag-and-drop it onto the QGIS map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Drag-and-drop it onto the QGIS map canvas.

trans-unit.source: Open NetCDF raster files
trans-unit.target: Open NetCDF raster files

trans-unit.source: `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Raster Layer` → Select your file → click `Add`.
trans-unit.target: `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Raster Layer` → Select your file → click `Add`.

trans-unit.source: A window will open and you have to select the exact dataset you want to use → Click `Add Layers`
trans-unit.target: A window will open and you have to select the exact dataset you want to use → Click `Add Layers`

trans-unit.source: Click on the `?` in the Layers window. The window `Coordination Reference System Select` will open → Select the correct reference system → Click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Click on the `?` in the Layers window. The window `Coordination Reference System Select` will open → Select the correct reference system → Click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Text data import
trans-unit.target: Text data import

trans-unit.source: Open .csv data in QGIS
trans-unit.target: Open .csv data in QGIS

trans-unit.source: :::{mote} When loading vector data in text format like .csv or .txt in QGIS, this data has to have latitude and longitude columns.
trans-unit.target: :::{mote} When loading vector data in text format like .csv or .txt in QGIS, this data has to have latitude and longitude columns.

trans-unit.source: `X field` = “LONGITUDE”
trans-unit.target: `X field` = “LONGITUDE”

trans-unit.source: `Y field` = “LATITUDE” :::
trans-unit.target: `Y field` = “LATITUDE” :::

trans-unit.source: `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Open Delimited Text Layer`.
trans-unit.target: `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Open Delimited Text Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Click on `File name` click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to your csv. file and click `Open`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `File name` click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) and navigate to your csv. file and click `Open`.

trans-unit.source: In the window "Data Source manager| Delimited Text" in QGIS you can find multiple drop-down menus
trans-unit.target: In the window "Data Source manager| Delimited Text" in QGIS you can find multiple drop-down menus

trans-unit.source: `File Format`: Here you can specify which delimiter is used in the file you want to import. In a standard `.csv` file commas `,` is used. If this is not the case, select `Custom delimiters`. Here you can choose the exact delimiter used in your file. :::{tip} To find out which delimiter is used you can open your .csv file in Notepad or Excel. There you can check which delimiter is used to separate the information. :::
trans-unit.target: `File Format`: Here you can specify which delimiter is used in the file you want to import. In a standard `.csv` file commas `,` is used. If this is not the case, select `Custom delimiters`. Here you can choose the exact delimiter used in your file. :::{tip} To find out which delimiter is used you can open your .csv file in Notepad or Excel. There you can check which delimiter is used to separate the information. :::

trans-unit.source: `Record and Fields Options`: Under this drop-down menu, you can instruct QGIS to detect the data type of the different columns of the field and to detect column headers. Usually, you do not have to make any adjustments here.
trans-unit.target: `Record and Fields Options`: Under this drop-down menu, you can instruct QGIS to detect the data type of the different columns of the field and to detect column headers. Usually, you do not have to make any adjustments here.

trans-unit.source: `Geometry definition`: In this section, you specify which columns of the file contain the spatial information to geo-referenced the data on the map. If the file has a column containing __latitude__ and another with __longitude__ data, you can use them to georeferenced the data. Check `Point Coordinates`. Select for `X field` “LONGITUDE” and for `Y field` “LATITUDE”. Under `Geometry CRS`select the coordinate reference system (CRS). By default, QGIS will select the CRS of the project. If the file does not have spatial information choose the option `No geometry (attribute only table)`.
trans-unit.target: `Geometry definition`: In this section, you specify which columns of the file contain the spatial information to geo-referenced the data on the map. If the file has a column containing __latitude__ and another with __longitude__ data, you can use them to georeferenced the data. Check `Point Coordinates`. Select for `X field` “LONGITUDE” and for `Y field` “LATITUDE”. Under `Geometry CRS`select the coordinate reference system (CRS). By default, QGIS will select the CRS of the project. If the file does not have spatial information choose the option `No geometry (attribute only table)`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Add`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Add`.

trans-unit.source: Open .xlsx files in QGIS
trans-unit.target: Open .xlsx files in QGIS

trans-unit.source: Drag and drop the .xlsx file in QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Drag and drop the .xlsx file in QGIS.

trans-unit.source: If the file contains multiple tables, select the table you want to work with. Click `Add Layers`.
trans-unit.target: If the file contains multiple tables, select the table you want to work with. Click `Add Layers`.

trans-unit.source: click on the `Processing` tab → `Toolbox` → search for the tool `Create points layer from table`.
trans-unit.target: click on the `Processing` tab → `Toolbox` → search for the tool `Create points layer from table`.

trans-unit.source: Select you table as `Input Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Select you table as `Input Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Select the longitude column for `X field` and the latitude column for `Y field`.
trans-unit.target: Select the longitude column for `X field` and the latitude column for `Y field`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.
